title: EOI Points Calculator for Skilled Migration to Australia
subtitle: Visa subclasses 189 · 190 · 491 — compare multiple skills assessments side by side
sharedNote: These belong to you as the applicant and apply to every assessment: age, English, qualification, partner status, plus STEM, Australian study, regional study and NAATI bonuses.
jobsNote: An applicant may hold several skills assessments. Work experience and the Professional Year must relate to the nominated occupation, so they are entered per occupation.

boxes.stem: STEM / IT Masters by research or PhD (2 yrs)

pyLabel: Professional Year (Accounting / Eng / IT, related to this occupation)
noBestPath: Select a nominated occupation to compute visa pathways
pathNote: 189 requires an MLTSSL occupation. 190 requires state nomination (+5 pts) and accepts MLTSSL / STSOL. 491 requires state or family regional nomination (+15 pts) and accepts all lists including ROL. Every pathway needs 65+ points to lodge an EOI. Note: each state also keeps its own 190/491 occupation list — the tags on the right show which states can nominate your chosen occupation. See "State list differences" under Reference.
statesIntro: Federal lists (MLTSSL / STSOL / ROL) are only the entry ticket: each state and territory keeps its own occupation list, selection method and extra requirements (residence, local work, higher English or points), adjusted through the year. 20,350 nomination places nationally in 2025–26. Once you pick an occupation, the states that can nominate it are tagged below.
statesNote: A 2025–26 snapshot — always check each state's official page.

NSW.how: 190 Priority Skilled Occupation List (High / Med / Low tiers)
NSW.tip: NSW work valued; ICT, health, engineering, construction first

VIC.how: No fixed list · ROI selection (Live in Melbourne)
VIC.tip: Health, teaching, STEM prioritised; local work & study count

QLD.tip: Onshore usually needs 6+ months of QLD work

WA.tip: General stream often needs a 6-month WA contract; mining, construction, health

SA.how: Broadest state list (hundreds of onshore roles)
SA.tip: Per-occupation extra requirements; monthly invitation data

TAS.tip: Usually 6–9 months of TAS work; settlement focus

ACT.how: ACT Nominated Migration Program Occupation List + Canberra Matrix
ACT.tip: Canberra residence & work history required; mostly local invitees

NT.how: Offshore MOL · onshore needs NT connection
NT.tip: Very limited places in 2025–26, exceptions only

sug.stem: Complete a STEM research Masters or PhD
sug.professionalYear: Complete a Professional Year for this occupation
sug.ausStudy: Complete 2 years of full-time study in Australia
sug.mltssl: Add an MLTSSL occupation assessment to unlock subclass 189

roundsNote: Subclass 189 now runs a few large, roughly quarterly rounds ranked by occupation. 65 points is only the floor for high-demand trades; professional, STEM and medical occupations typically needed 85–115 points in the same round.
tlNote: The projection is generated from the months you enter next to each field above (birth, English test, work start, assessment issue) — age-bracket drops, work milestones and credential expiries over the next 5 years. Assumes continuous full-time work from each start month.
tlNaatiHint: Enabled with the NAATI bonus above
tlFutureBirth: Birth month must be in the past
tlOver45: Age 45+ — no longer eligible for these visas
tlEmpty: Enter a month next to any field above (birth month, work start month…) to draw the projection.
tlChartSummary: Projected score timeline from {{from}} to {{to}}, starting at {{score}} points with {{n}} events.
disclaimer: This calculator is for reference only. Immigration rules change frequently — always verify with the Department of Home Affairs or a registered migration agent.
cardFoot: Reference only · verify with the Department of Home Affairs
